meta.title: Consolidated SRT Lab intel — Security bytes, Module syncing, Key programming

interpretation.27 04: Send key level 4 / request seed level 4 alt (FCA-specific)

security_access_frames_extracted.send_key_frames_absent: Key send frames (27 02, 27 06, 27 62, 27 66) are NOT in IL literals — they carry computed key bytes from W6/W7/XTEA/ht cipher output at runtime.

cipher_dispatch.family_wrapper_names: 10 family entries: family_31, family_39, family_17, family_21, family_22, family_27, family_37, family_66 (each maps levels 1/3/5/7 to Dotfuscator wrapper method names like jh/jg/jf, c2/cz/cw, tv/tu/tt, etc.)

cipher_methods_in_il.w6_cipher_core: Method[204] w6 (138 bytes IL) — linear bit-shuffle: shl/shr/and 0xFF / and 0xFFFF / xor / add. Returns 2 bytes via conv.u1 x2.
cipher_methods_in_il.w7_cipher_harness: Method[203] w7 (765 bytes IL) — loads 23 decrypted constants, chains 6+ BigInteger helper methods.
cipher_methods_in_il.w7_cipher_init: Method[1140] .cctor (378 bytes IL) — initializes cipher constants via newobj BigInteger.ctor + InitializeArray. 12 byte-array constants extracted from .data section.
cipher_methods_in_il.dotfuscator_decrypt: Method[26] h (110 bytes IL) — ToCharArray + per-char XOR with running key starting 0x6DDC67B5+salt; byte-swap; Intern result.

most_suspicious.4083618902: In Method[1436] zz = SendCodeCardLogin, paired with 27 04 frame — possible CodeCard auth payload
most_suspicious.3E07860DAD: In Method[1436] zz = SendCodeCardLogin, paired with 27 04 00 frame — possible CodeCard auth payload

real_candidate_5byte_hex_strings.interpretation_caveat: Most 5-byte hex strings are structured data records (ECU type IDs, calibration codes), NOT crypto keys. The grouped prefixes (32 83 XX, 34 80 XX, 7A 07 XX, B3 86 XX) suggest part-number families or display records. Only the zz/SendCodeCardLogin pair is high-confidence key-related.

security_access_outcome_messages.wrong_security_access_after_seed_request: Some procedures fail with this when the seed-response math is wrong

tier1_routine_descriptions.1126: This routine is used to allow BCM to transfer secret keys to ECM and ESL operating on the protection of the BCM. Login with Code Card before running this procedure.
tier1_routine_descriptions.1520: RAM only. Use this function when the BCM has been replaced. It will create the BCM backup of the secret key value stored in the RF-HUB.
tier1_routine_descriptions.1750: This routine is used to allow BCM to transfer secret keys to ECM/PCM operating on the protection of the BCM. Use it when replacing the ECM/PCM.
tier1_routine_descriptions.1751: This routine is used to allow BCM to transfer secret keys to ESL operating on the protection of the BCM. Use it when replacing the ESL.
tier1_routine_descriptions.2508: governor output duty cycle for LSU heater 0
tier1_routine_descriptions.1367: RAM trucks only with high-line system. Use this function to program FOBIK(s) when the RF-HUB has been replaced or additional FOBIK(s) need to be added. The FOBIK(s) being pRograMmEd MUST bE

sgw_unlock_required_2018plus.from_cda: CDA.swf strings confirm: Authenticated Diagnostics is required for any vehicle equipped with a Security Gateway (SGW). 2018+ vehicles require SGW unlock before any Tier-1 routine.
sgw_unlock_required_2018plus.flow: 1) CDA -> Stellantis US backend login -> SGW unlock token. 2) Bench bypass: physical PIN dongle (DongleSecurityGatewayMessage) -> CDA -> Stellantis backend -> SGW unlock token.

uds_flow: 1. 27 03 (SecurityAccess Request Seed level 3) -> ECU returns 8-byte seed
2. AlfaOBD computes key from seed using cipher (W6/W7/XTEA/ht depending on ECU family + level)
4. ECU validates; on success returns 67 04 positive response
5. 31 01 02 11 (RoutineControl Start, RID=0x0211, vehicle config backup write) — or whatever routine the operator selected
codecard_flow.interpretation_caveat: The 5-digit USER input gets transformed (likely hashed/padded/derived) into the 5-byte payload sent in 27 04. The hex constants 4083618902 and 3E07860DAD in IL are NOT raw user CodeCards — they look like sample/test values baked into the binary OR expected-response patterns.

states.PIN login failed. Function is not suppor: ECU does not support PIN-based auth

skim_immobilizer.description: Sentry Key Immobilizer Module — FCA anti-theft system, paired with engine ECM/PCM.
skim_immobilizer.immobilizer_protocol: Immobilizer HCP-CBC Handshake (HCP = Honda Crypto Protocol or FCA-Custom Protocol; CBC = Cipher Block Chaining).
skim_immobilizer.fobik_count_supported: Up to 8 FOBIKs paired per vehicle (FOBIK #1 through FOBIK #8).
skim_immobilizer.rfh_to_skim_relationship: RFH (Radio Frequency Hub) reads FOBIK transponder, communicates with SKIM/WIN over CAN. BCM stores the secret key shared with RFH for vehicle-specific authentication.
